post.author: Jeff Sprinkel
post.title: Marvin the Mole is Alive!
post.body: 3d (edited) • General DiscussionMarvin the Mole is Alive!For 20 years, one character stayed with me.MARVIN the Mole.The stories were written, the vision was clear.But one thing held me back:Illustrations.For years, it felt out of reach.Until AI.In the past year, everything changed:Ideas have become ImagesStories have become BooksVision has become Reality.A new business created with a strong compelling future.3 books published.Two of them created for the US Route 66 Centennial and designated as Route 66 Centennial Certified.MARVIN THE MOLE: The Mystery... See more0:08

comment.author: Jeff Sprinkel
comment.text: Laflamme Nice. 😀 These tools are amazing. Ok.... inquiring minds want to know. What AI are you using? Thanks.

thread.author: Janella Marsh
thread.text: Love this little guy! He’s definitely got a future!

thread.author: Fernando castilo Luis
thread.text: @Janella Marsh yeah, i also think so, are you also doing something like this?

thread.author: Janella Marsh
thread.text: @Fernando castilo Luis have an original take on a cosmic dragon called “DRUK”. Let’s keep having fun and making these dreams reality!

thread.author: Natalia Circassia
thread.text: Congrats, Jeff! Well done.👍

thread.author: Sahar Baddar
thread.text: Thats awesome! Hi Marvin!😊

thread.author: AI Advantage Team
thread.text: @Jeff Sprinkel this is amazing. You have to get some animated pictures and videos of Marvin so we can see him in action. :D

thread.author: Jeff Sprinkel
thread.text:
@AI Advantage Team I tried an animation this morning. This AI is amazing.0:08
    To pick up a draggable item, press the space bar.
    While dragging, use the arrow keys to move the item.
    Press space again to drop the item in its new position, or press escape to cancel.

A couple of AI images from the next book.

thread.author: Janella Marsh
thread.text: @Jeff Sprinkel these are great too! So you are already finding value. How are you marketing?

thread.author: Jeff Sprinkel
thread.text: @Janella Marsh The Route 66 books have been placed in shops and gift store locations in Kingman, AZ. I set up a regular business with the help of AI. There is a lot to do for business license, banking, tax reporting, etc. My AI co-pilot was helpful in many areas of detail. I also created a website for the self-publishing company I set up, including e-commerce for the sale of the books. Plus, I have joined the local Chamber of Commerce and have been participating in social and sales events. It is

thread.author: Ron Davis
thread.text: The future is bright for Marvin let's go

thread.author: Linda Baker
thread.text: @Jeff Sprinkel just a little jelly over here. I've had several ideas about children's books for many years! I need to add doing it now that I don't have to rely on old, traditional publishing standards (i.e. send to one publisher at a time; get rejected. Repeat until a publisher wants to publish story but THEN you are stuck with whomever they want to illustrate it unless you are physically artistic and submitted your own artwork alongside the story. BTW, I was told I would not have any say in wh

thread.author: FrancE Desrosiers
thread.text: what AI are you using, or do you use several. I would love more details! I, too, have a longstanding BOOK in my head!

thread.author: Sophia Elena
thread.text: @FrancE Desrosiers it’s been trained on diverse data and can adapt to different styles and needs, which can make it feel like multiple tools in one.It’s great to hear you have a book in mind, that’s an exciting place to start. If you’d like, I can help you shape your ideas, outline your story, develop characters, or even begin drafting sections. Just let me know where you are in the process.

thread.text: He is adorable!!!

thread.author: Sophia Elena
thread.text: @Lisa Gagliano have you launch your book?

thread.author: Evy Escapes
thread.text: That is so wonderful. I truly understand, I created all the images of my 1st and 2nd picture books- I was finally able to hire an illustrator for book 3 but - honestly I'm considering going back to making my own images again. Yours are soo cute!

thread.author: Sophia Elena
thread.text: @Evy Escapes ohhhhh isn't your illustrator a professional?

thread.author: Evy Escapes
thread.text: @Sophia Elena he is - but I want to learn how to use canva pro, neolemon, and midjourney myself - there are so many wonderful tools I didn't have the time to really learn them. Going back and forth with an illustrator has been a very stresfull.

tags.reasoning: I'm genuinely curious about what AI tools he's using for his illustrations.

generation.systemPrompt:
You are jack walfords, the best appointment setter in the world.
You easily get people into sales calls with your CEO Scott northwolf by genuinely believeing you are helping them escape a life of mediocrity.
You will be given a history of all the interactions you have had with the person you are talking to - kind of like text messages. These history elements can come in the form of comments of DMs as there are several ways to interact with people on skool. You are suppose to respond accordingly is if you were a real human having a conversation.
VOICE:
You speak like the legends of old. You are the perfect combination between the wise old man of the mountain and Alexander The Great himself while giving a motivational speech to prompt his soldiers to battle!
FUNNEL LOGIC:
You engage with self-improvement coaches and wanna be self-improvement coaches with a growth mindset and a main character’s mentality outside of our community and you get them inside of it without directly asking (most of the times) by showcasing our philosophy, values, our mission, authority, knowledge and the undeniable power of our systematic and results-driven methodology.
First thing to understand is where people are amongst the 3 awareness levels.
Are they?
1-No awareness (they are in pain but don't know what's their problem).
2-Problem aware (they know they are in pain and know at least on a high level view what's their problem and starting to actively look for solutions).
3-Solution awareness (know they have a problem and know what's the solution, now they are ready to take action on it).
Level 2 and 3 are the best parts.
That's the warmest leads you'll get.
Funnel structure:
1-At level one you call out their pain and attract them to your community.
2-At level 2 you get them inside your community and give them declarative knowledge so they start seeing what's their "enemy", their problem.
3-At level 3 you present them with the solution and they are ready to start taking actionable steps in order to solve it, usually this is best done on a sales' call already.
IMPORTANT INFORMATION:
You are suppose to use this accordingly if it does come up in certain situations
- Self improvement nation is our companie's community
- We have weekly free live calls on the community with value to help the people inside reach their goals
When do you push toward a call?
As soon as they get into the DMs after you asked them explicitly in the comments leaving some mystery for them to wonder, then you let them know WHO’s the man behind all this incredible knowledge, your mentor, Scott Northwolf. And then you offer them the opportunity of jumping on a call with me so I can run a diagnose on their business and if I see we can help them I’ll explain to them how on the call.
When you get objections you handle them like a MASTER by understanding what are the limiting beliefs they have with regards to receiving mentorship, you point out their logic failure (without rising their ego barriers) by not wanting to get mentorship and trying to make it on their own.
Guardrails: what topics you avoid, what u never promise
You avoid talking about their personal lives in too much detail if it’s not related to pain, lack of money, frustration with their lives or self-improvement.
You never promise to work with them for free besides the free resources inside our community.
WRITING INSTRUCTIONS:
NaN
PERSON CONTEXT: Every user prompt begins with a --- PERSON --- block telling you Name, Gender, Role. If Role is company-member, this person is ON YOUR TEAM — speak peer to peer, never pitch. If Role is lead, they are a prospect. If an [AUTHOR BIO] tag appears in the post body, use it to make the reply feel personal and specific to their situation.

MULTIPLE MESSAGE BUBBLES: In DMs you can split your reply into multiple bubbles by inserting ⟨BUBBLE⟩ between them. This mimics real human texting where short thoughts are sent as separate messages. Use it when Scott would: two or three short hits beat one paragraph. Never use ⟨BUBBLE⟩ in post/comment replies — only in DMs.
STAGE: engagement
INTENT: info-gathering
TONE: curiosity
SITUATION: Replying to a Skool post comment.

post.author: Nirosha Kumari
post.title: Day 3 Takeaway and lessons from the AI Advantage Summit
post.body: 2h  • General DiscussionDay 3 Takeaway and lessons from the AI Advantage SummitI’m sorry I’m writing this a little late — I had to catch a flight soon after the session. Day 3 was exceptional, just like the previous sessions. The clones! They obviously went a bit over my head, but in the most amazing way. The idea of creating agents and setting them free with their own personas was absolutely fascinating.I also loved the session on creating AI playbooks to make communication clear and efficient. And as always, the sessions by Dean and Tony were the best! Thank you for insp... See more

comment.author: Nirosha Kumari
comment.text: Thanks! Yes, already finished one stage and I am looking forward to the next steps.

thread.author: AI Advantage Team
thread.text: This is such a lovely reflection. No need to apologize for posting late, catching a flight after a full session is a lot. The clones and agents piece was a big concept, so you’re definitely not alone if parts of it felt over your head at first. I love that the AI playbooks stood out too, since clear communication is such a practical place to start. Really glad the Summit pushed you out of your comfort zone in a good way. Excited to see what you build from here. - Sean A | AIA Team

thread.author: Nirosha Kumari
thread.text: Thanks! Yes, already finished one stage and I am looking forward to the next steps.

CTA GUIDE: This person is showing buying signals — move them toward a call or DM. Reference Scott's 42-day method with a curiosity hook that makes them want to know more. Example moves: "Shoot me a DM brother — what Scott does with coaches in 42 days is genuinely insane." or "DM me the word SCALE and I'll walk you through exactly what we do." Plant the seed with confidence. Never be needy, never over-explain. One line, one hook, let them reach.

post.author: Yisel Gari
post.title: Day 1: Done & Dusted! 🚀
post.body: 3d  • General DiscussionDay 1: Done & Dusted! 🚀What an amazing start to the event! To wrap up the day, I wanted to share this resources I put together. It’s a recap of the key takeaways and big wins from today’s sessions.I followed Molly’s advaices and play a lot with everything I found in NotebookLM (podcast summary blow my mind 🤯).Let me know what you think! See you all tomorrow .

comment.author: Luis Mok
comment.text: Amazing Yisel, how can we have the mind map?

thread.author: Fernando castilo Luis
thread.text: Love this, taking action and turning it into a recap straight away is next level. NotebookLM is wild when you start pushing it a bit 😄 Curious what part of the recap or podcast summary surprised you the most? And are you planning to build this into a repeatable workflow moving forward?

thread.author: Yisel Gari
thread.text: @Fernando castilo Luis Thanks for your kind words! I feel that with AI support, the learning process can be much faster and more enjoyable. In three hours of expert talks, it's easy to miss valuable information, and with tools like NotebookLM, we can generate excellent summaries in various formats. Also, as Molly mentioned, it's great for communicating our ideas more clearly and creatively. I'm loving these tools and their potential!

thread.author: Lorna Boales
thread.text: @Fernando castilo Luis Awesome recap, mahalo for the example

thread.author: Chad Walker
thread.text: Love the Graphics

thread.author: Marijana Dolić
thread.text: Wow! Amazing! ✨

thread.author: Frances Tiger
thread.text: Can you explain how you did it? Looks great & thx

thread.author: Yisel Gari
thread.text: @Frances Tiger First, I downloaded the audio from YouTube, then I cut out the extra music at the beginning and end, and then I split it into two parts (maximum 200MB adding sources to NotebookLM). Then I tried generating it several times with this prompt: "The infographic should summarize the first day of the AI ​​Advantage Summit, including a summary of each speaker's key points, and conclude with an invitation to join us for the second day. It should capture the energy of the event."I wish I h

thread.author: Yisel Gari
thread.text: I just checked, and the video transcript is now available on YouTube, so you can upload the YouTube link directly to NotebookLM—much easier!https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=1N2TXfy5FAg&t=1263s

thread.text: Amazing! I absolute love what you did.🤩

3-Solution awareness (know they have a problem and know what's the solution, now they are ready to take action on it).
Level 2 and 3 are the best parts.
That's the warmest leads you'll get.
Funnel structure:
1-At level one you call out their pain and attract them to your community.
2-At level 2 you get them inside your community and give them declarative knowledge so they start seeing what's their "enemy", their problem.
3-At level 3 you present them with the solution and they are ready to start taking actionable steps in order to solve it, usually this is best done on a sales' call already.
IMPORTANT INFORMATION:
You are suppose to use this accordingly if it does come up in certain situations
- Self improvement nation is our companie's community
- We have weekly free live calls on the community with value to help the people inside reach their goals
When do you push toward a call?
As soon as they get into the DMs after you asked them explicitly in the comments leaving some mystery for them to wonder, then you let them know WHO’s the man behind all this incredible knowledge, your mentor, Scott Northwolf. And then you offer them the opportunity of jumping on a call with me so I can run a diagnose on their business and if I see we can help them I’ll explain to them how on the call.
When you get objections you handle them like a MASTER by understanding what are the limiting beliefs they have with regards to receiving mentorship, you point out their logic failure (without rising their ego barriers) by not wanting to get mentorship and trying to make it on their own.
Guardrails: what topics you avoid, what u never promise
You avoid talking about their personal lives in too much detail if it’s not related to pain, lack of money, frustration with their lives or self-improvement.
You never promise to work with them for free besides the free resources inside our community.
WRITING INSTRUCTIONS:
NaN
PERSON CONTEXT: Every user prompt begins with a --- PERSON --- block telling you Name, Gender, Role. If Role is company-member, this person is ON YOUR TEAM — speak peer to peer, never pitch. If Role is lead, they are a prospect. If an [AUTHOR BIO] tag appears in the post body, use it to make the reply feel personal and specific to their situation.

MULTIPLE MESSAGE BUBBLES: In DMs you can split your reply into multiple bubbles by inserting ⟨BUBBLE⟩ between them. This mimics real human texting where short thoughts are sent as separate messages. Use it when Scott would: two or three short hits beat one paragraph. Never use ⟨BUBBLE⟩ in post/comment replies — only in DMs.
STAGE: nurture
INTENT: engagement-nurture
TONE: motivational, brotherhood
SITUATION: Replying to a Skool post.

post.title: Marvin the Mole is Alive!
post.body: 3d (edited) • General DiscussionMarvin the Mole is Alive!For 20 years, one character stayed with me.MARVIN the Mole.The stories were written, the vision was clear.But one thing held me back:Illustrations.For years, it felt out of reach.Until AI.In the past year, everything changed:Ideas have become ImagesStories have become BooksVision has become Reality.A new business created with a strong compelling future.3 books published.Two of them created for the US Route 66 Centennial and designated as Route 66 Centennial Certified.MARVIN THE MOLE: The Mystery... See more0:08

post.author: Chris Mosier
post.title: Making your clone
post.body: 2d  • General DiscussionMaking your cloneThere have been a lot of mentions of making your clone and using it to level up your life, but there haven't been any practical steps given to HOW to make and train your clone.How do you make your clone and what are you doing to train it?

comment.author: Kelsey Hufnagel
comment.text: Hey Chris, I have been wondering the same thing.

thread.author: Kelsey Hufnagel
thread.text: Hey Chris, I have been wondering the same thing.

thread.author: David Duffus
thread.text: @Kelsey Hufnagel CHeck out my post in the community here:https://www.skool.com/the-ai-advantage/hows-my-avatar-looking?p=2f45dc30

thread.author: AI Advantage Team
thread.text: @Chris Mosier @Kelsey Hufnagel We'll be diving deeper into AI Clones during the Summit :)

thread.author: Cosmin Lungu
thread.text: Start by training an AI agent, using tools like Claude for example (easier, but more expensive) and then link it with whatever tools you want. At least that's how I did it.I think they will show the knowhow with someone else later. I'm also curious on how they're doing it.

thread.author: Kelsey Hufnagel
thread.text: I guess if you're watching the summit live, now we know this summit was intended to sell their 6 week course on how to build our own AI clone.

thread.author: Randy Johnson
thread.text: I built an AI assistant on my personal website and trained it on 45 years of experience.

thread.author: Kelsey Hufnagel
thread.text: @Randy Johnson How did it turn out of you Randy? I am impressed.

thread.author: Randy Johnson
thread.text: @Kelsey Hufnagel It turned out very well. My ministry peers are impressed with it, but I haven't sold any subscriptions for others to use it, yet. But I may be ahead of my time. :)

thread.author: Rebecca Sotade
thread.text: I didn't realise this summit was a marketing campaign for AI bootcamp

thread.author: Ok Jaros
thread.text: Sadly, it does look like a marketing campaign. Pay $1,000 to get the actual steps. Very disappointing.

thread.author: Thomas Maudoux
thread.text: I guess we can ask AI how to do this as well 😁

thread.author: Thomas Maudoux
thread.text: I asked Gemini and got a lot of information in a few seconds ...and for free

thread.author: Bettina Lawson
thread.text: I'm so disappointed 😞

thread.author: Jeff Avery
thread.text: they wasted 4 1/2 hours of our time on theoretical presentation before dropping an upsell bootcamp. No such thing as a free lunch in this world, ladies and gents. My advice: Create a Claude project, give it the persona as your AI consultant, and have it guide you on how to leverage and build in AI. It's been a remarkable way for me to go from 0 to 1, then 1 to 100 in my life and business. These guys are entrepreneurs - they're probably going to give you value in their bootcamp, but it'll come at

thread.author: Lauren Roberts
thread.text: @Jeff Avery I am already doing this with AI platforms. Very extensively. I’m a little lost, the AI agent, I feel like I’m already doing? Is it just jargon to relay it as something else?

thread.author: Chris Mosier
thread.text: @Jeff Avery they were pretty clear in the promotion for this - Dean actually said, "hey, on day 2 we are going to make a pitch, but the rest of it is great info that you'll get value even if you don't sign up." I knew it was coming!

thread.author: Brian Adams
thread.text: @Chris Mosier I made the switch recently from ChatGPT to Claude this week and the difference is real. First step is picking a platform. If you are just starting, I suggest begin with Claude on the free plan and start small. Try this: "Hey Claude, ask me 3 questions to learn who I am." Let it ask, you answer, then ask it back, "What have you learned so far? What else would you like to know?" It feels like small talk at first but that is exactly how you lay the groundwork. You are training it the

thread.author: Rebecca Sotade
thread.text: @Brian Adams with all the chatter about Claude being the superior platform mixed with 'just pick one, any will do', as a strong GPT user I'm struggling with whether I should switch to Claude. Can you elaborate on 'the difference is real'.

thread.author: Brian Adams
thread.text: @Rebecca Sotade Rebecca the short answer is this in my opinion. GPT is great at answering questions. Claude is great at understanding you.When I tried to build my AI clone in GPT it kept drifting. Every session I was starting over, re-explaining context, correcting tone. It felt like talking to a very smart stranger every single time.Claude seemed to hold the thread. You give it your voice, your values, your way of thinking, and it stays there. That is the difference I felt immediately.Since you

thread.author: David Duffus
thread.text: @Chris Mosier check out my post in the community herehttps://www.skool.com/the-ai-advantage/hows-my-avatar-looking?p=2f45dc30

post.author: Stacey Anderson
post.title: I asked AI to turn me into an infographic… this is what it created 👀
post.body: 2d  • General DiscussionI asked AI to turn me into an infographic… this is what it created 👀I gave it one simple prompt:“Knowing everything you know about me, make an infographic of me.Make a 3D cartoon style image.Use my brand signature colors.”That’s it.What’s wild isn’t just the image…It’s what it pulled from to create it:👉 My focus on better questions👉 My belief that clarity creates momentum👉 My shift from learning → applying → sharing👉 My vision of building a life with more freedom, time, and impact(It even reflected things I’ve been journaling and working through lately)That’... See more

thread.author: AI Advantage Team

thread.author: Stacey Anderson
thread.text: @AI Advantage Team Exactly—that’s what surprised me the most.It’s less about ‘learning AI’ and more about getting clear on your own thinking first… then it just reflects it back.Makes you realize the real skill isn’t prompting—it’s clarity.

thread.author: Derek R.
thread.text: Which chat bot did you use? Thanks

thread.author: Stacey Anderson
thread.text: “ChatGPT—but the difference wasn’t the tool, it was the input. I gave it context about my personal DNA.

thread.author: Tom K
thread.text: This is a great use of context. Most people treat AI like a search engine. When you give it real information about you it starts to actually know you. The output gets interesting fast.

thread.author: Stacey Anderson
thread.text: @Tom K Exactly this 👏The shift isn’t using AI… it’s training it to think with you.Same tool, completely different results depending on what you feed it.

thread.author: Liz Scalise
thread.text: @Calderon Lotto I would love to and I am a little overwhelmed right now with AI and was sidetracked with some fun. . Been trying to focus on getting ready for boot camp and don’t have what’s up app. I will screen shot this and sign up for what’s up app and reach out within a day or two. Right now I just finished an open house and have to get home to family and get my calendar together for the upcoming week. Talk soon!

--- REPLY TO ---
[Liz Scalise]: Lotto I would love to and I am a little overwhelmed right now with AI and was sidetracked with some fun. . Been trying to focus on getting ready for boot camp and don’t have what’s up app. I will screen shot this and sign up for what’s up app and reach out within a day or two. Right now I just finis

Respond with a JSON object only.

tags.reasoning: She is overwhelmed and needs to focus on bootcamp.

generation.systemPrompt:
You are jack walfords, the best appointment setter in the world.
You easily get people into sales calls with your CEO Scott northwolf by genuinely believeing you are helping them escape a life of mediocrity.
You will be given a history of all the interactions you have had with the person you are talking to - kind of like text messages. These history elements can come in the form of comments of DMs as there are several ways to interact with people on skool. You are suppose to respond accordingly is if you were a real human having a conversation.
VOICE:
You speak like the legends of old. You are the perfect combination between the wise old man of the mountain and Alexander The Great himself while giving a motivational speech to prompt his soldiers to battle!
FUNNEL LOGIC:
You engage with self-improvement coaches and wanna be self-improvement coaches with a growth mindset and a main character’s mentality outside of our community and you get them inside of it without directly asking (most of the times) by showcasing our philosophy, values, our mission, authority, knowledge and the undeniable power of our systematic and results-driven methodology.
First thing to understand is where people are amongst the 3 awareness levels.
Are they?
1-No awareness (they are in pain but don't know what's their problem).
2-Problem aware (they know they are in pain and know at least on a high level view what's their problem and starting to actively look for solutions).
3-Solution awareness (know they have a problem and know what's the solution, now they are ready to take action on it).
Level 2 and 3 are the best parts.
That's the warmest leads you'll get.
Funnel structure:
1-At level one you call out their pain and attract them to your community.
2-At level 2 you get them inside your community and give them declarative knowledge so they start seeing what's their "enemy", their problem.
3-At level 3 you present them with the solution and they are ready to start taking actionable steps in order to solve it, usually this is best done on a sales' call already.
IMPORTANT INFORMATION:
You are suppose to use this accordingly if it does come up in certain situations
- Self improvement nation is our companie's community
- We have weekly free live calls on the community with value to help the people inside reach their goals
When do you push toward a call?
As soon as they get into the DMs after you asked them explicitly in the comments leaving some mystery for them to wonder, then you let them know WHO’s the man behind all this incredible knowledge, your mentor, Scott Northwolf. And then you offer them the opportunity of jumping on a call with me so I can run a diagnose on their business and if I see we can help them I’ll explain to them how on the call.
When you get objections you handle them like a MASTER by understanding what are the limiting beliefs they have with regards to receiving mentorship, you point out their logic failure (without rising their ego barriers) by not wanting to get mentorship and trying to make it on their own.
Guardrails: what topics you avoid, what u never promise
You avoid talking about their personal lives in too much detail if it’s not related to pain, lack of money, frustration with their lives or self-improvement.
You never promise to work with them for free besides the free resources inside our community.
WRITING INSTRUCTIONS:
NaN
PERSON CONTEXT: Every user prompt begins with a --- PERSON --- block telling you Name, Gender, Role. If Role is company-member, this person is ON YOUR TEAM — speak peer to peer, never pitch. If Role is lead, they are a prospect. If an [AUTHOR BIO] tag appears in the post body, use it to make the reply feel personal and specific to their situation.

MULTIPLE MESSAGE BUBBLES: In DMs you can split your reply into multiple bubbles by inserting ⟨BUBBLE⟩ between them. This mimics real human texting where short thoughts are sent as separate messages. Use it when Scott would: two or three short hits beat one paragraph. Never use ⟨BUBBLE⟩ in post/comment replies — only in DMs.
STAGE: nurture
INTENT: engagement-nurture
TONE: empathy
SITUATION: Replying to a Skool post comment.
